bead.title: fizeau: import agent-3bb96bf5 release once tagged (Role + CorrelationID + RoutingActual.Power)
bead.description:
Cross-repo coordination bead. Upstream Fizeau work is filed at agent-3bb96bf5 (epic) in /home/erik/Projects/agent. That epic adds three things to CONTRACT-003: Role string + CorrelationID string on ExecuteRequest + RouteRequest (pure observability — Day 1 no routing effect), and Power int on ServiceRoutingActual (so DDx can compute next-attempt MinPower without importing catalog code).

NOT BLOCKED ON A NEW LARGE CONTRACT EXTENSION. The previously-imagined ExcludePinned / ProviderID / ProviderClass / separate-review-cost fields turned out to be unnecessary — most of what we thought we needed was already shipped in fizeau v0.9.28 (MinPower/MaxPower request plumbing via agent-79e194aa closed; Power on ListModels via agent-da67ebbe closed; CostUSD on Final already present; full power-routing test contract via agent-de968c76 closed).

This bead's scope: (1) wait for agent-3bb96bf5 to ship a tagged release; (2) bump cli/go.mod to import that tagged version (NEVER go.mod replace per project memory); (3) verify Role / CorrelationID / RoutingActual.Power are accessible from DDx Go code.
bead.acceptance: 1. agent-3bb96bf5 tagged release identified (e.g., fizeau v0.10.0). 2. cli/go.mod updated to that tagged version. 3. cd cli && go build ./... succeeds. 4. cd cli && go test ./... passes (no test consumes the new fields yet — that's S10_2/3/5). 5. A trivial Go file references ExecuteRequest.Role and ServiceRoutingActual.Power to confirm symbols resolve.

events.summary: Reopening: title/description scope updated to 'wait for agent-3bb96bf5 tagged release + bump go.mod'. The drain closed this against the OLD large-contract-extension scope (correctly — that work was fictional). Under the new scope this stays open as an upstream-wait until agent-3bb96bf5 ships.
